title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: equilibrium point (also called fixed point steady state) autonomous ODE system state (x*,y*)(x^*, y^*) derivatives vanish simultaneously: f(x*,y*)=0,g(x*,y*)=0.f(x^*, y^*) = 0, \qquad g(x^*, y^*) = 0. Knowing location stability type equilibrium central understanding qualitative behavior system — tells us trajectories end , approach depart rest states, system responds perturbations. vignette covers: Finding equilibria numerically find_equilibrium() Jacobian matrix linearization Classifying equilibria classify_equilibrium() trace-determinant plane Drawing stable unstable manifolds gg_manifolds() complete worked example: nonlinear competition model Bifurcation preview: equilibrium type changes parameters
code: library(ggphasr) library(ggplot2)

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: simple systems, equilibria can often found hand. nonlinear systems, numerical root-finding reliable. find_equilibrium() uses Newton–Raphson iteration (via rootSolve::multiroot()) locate equilibria one starting points.

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: system several equilibria locations known advance, use y0 = NULL trigger automatic grid search. find_equilibrium() places starting points regular grid xlim × ylim, runs Newton–Raphson , deduplicates results: Technical aside: Newton–Raphson requires initial guess may converge non-equilibrium point fail converge starting point far root. find_equilibrium() guards verifying residual ∥f(x*)∥∞\|f(x^*)\|_\infty tolerance threshold accepting root. Results sorted x-coordinate deduplicated using Euclidean distance threshold (dedup_tol = 1e-4 default).
code: # example_11: dx/dt = x(3-x-2y), dy/dt = y(2-x-y) # Four equilibria: (0,0), (3,0), (0,2), (1,1) eq_list <- find_equilibrium(   ode_example_11,   y0     = NULL,   xlim   = c(0, 4),   ylim   = c(0, 3),   n_grid = 12L )  # Print each equilibrium rounded to 4 decimal places lapply(eq_list, round, digits = 4) #> [[1]] #> [1] 0 2 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 0 0 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 1 1 #>  #> [[4]] #> [1] 3 0

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: stability equilibrium (x*,y*)(x^*, y^*) determined behavior nearby solutions. Near equilibrium, nonlinear system d𝐲dt=𝐅(𝐲)\frac{d\mathbf{y}}{dt} = \mathbf{F}(\mathbf{y}) can approximated linearization: d𝛏dt≈J𝛏,\frac{d\boldsymbol{\xi}}{dt} \approx J\,\boldsymbol{\xi}, 𝛏=𝐲−𝐲*\boldsymbol{\xi} = \mathbf{y} - \mathbf{y}^* displacement equilibrium JJ Jacobian matrix: J=(∂f/∂x∂f/∂y∂g/∂x∂g/∂y)|(x*,y*).J = \begin{pmatrix} \partial f/\partial x & \partial f/\partial y \\ \partial g/\partial x & \partial g/\partial y \end{pmatrix}\bigg|_{(x^*, y^*)}. eigenvalues λ1,λ2\lambda_1, \lambda_2 JJ determine local behavior: Re(λi)<0\text{Re}(\lambda_i) < 0: equilibrium stable (trajectories approach ). Re(λi)>0\text{Re}(\lambda_i) > 0: equilibrium unstable (trajectories depart ). λ1<0<λ2\lambda_1 < 0 < \lambda_2 (one sign): equilibrium saddle. classify_equilibrium() computes Jacobian numerically via forward finite differences classifies equilibrium using trace-determinant criterion (see Section 4): eigenvalues λ=±i0.5≈±0.707i\lambda = \pm \sqrt{0.5} \approx \pm 0.707\,— purely imaginary, confirming center classification.
code: # Jacobian at the Lotka-Volterra interior equilibrium (2, 2) # Analytic Jacobian: [[0, -1], [0.5, 0]] -> tr = 0, det = 0.5 # -> Center (purely imaginary eigenvalues)  cl_interior <- classify_equilibrium(   ode_lotka_volterra,   equilibrium = eq_interior[[1L]],   parameters  = lv_params )  cl_interior[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det",                  "jacobian_11", "jacobian_12",                  "jacobian_21", "jacobian_22")] #>   x y classification tr det jacobian_11 jacobian_12 jacobian_21 jacobian_22 #> 1 2 2         Center  0   1           0          -1           1           0 cl_interior[, c("lambda_1_re", "lambda_1_im",                  "lambda_2_re", "lambda_2_im")] #>   lambda_1_re lambda_1_im lambda_2_re lambda_2_im #> 1           0           1           0          -1

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: Combining find_equilibrium() classify_equilibrium() via lapply() produces tidy summary table equilibria : table immediately reveals qualitative structure: origin coexistence point (1,1)(1, 1) saddles, (3,0)(3, 0) (0,2)(0, 2) stable single-species equilibria.
code: # All four equilibria of example_11 eq_table_11 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_example_11, equilibrium = eq) }))  eq_table_11[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det")] #>               x y classification        tr       det #> 1 -6.171961e-12 2    Stable node -3.000002  2.000003 #> 2  0.000000e+00 0  Unstable node  4.999998  5.999995 #> 3  1.000000e+00 1         Saddle -2.000002 -0.999998 #> 4  3.000000e+00 0    Stable node -4.000002  3.000004

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: 2D system, Jacobian two eigenvalues whose character completely determined trace τ=tr(J)\tau = \text{tr}(J) determinant Δ=det⁡(J)\Delta = \det(J): λ1,2=τ±τ2−4Δ2.\lambda_{1,2} = \frac{\tau \pm \sqrt{\tau^2 - 4\Delta}}{2}. classification rules : parabola τ2=4Δ\tau^2 = 4\Delta separates nodes spirals. can visualize tracking equilibrium type simple pendulum’s stable equilibrium origin moves trace-determinant plane damping coefficient bb increases:  bb increases 0, equilibrium moves center (τ=0\tau = 0, Δ>0\Delta > 0) stable spirals (τ<0\tau < 0, complex eigenvalues) crosses parabola τ2=4Δ\tau^2 = 4\Delta become stable node (real eigenvalues) — overdamped regime.
code:
b_vals   <- seq(0, 3, by = 0.15)  td_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(b_vals, function(b) {   result    <- classify_equilibrium(     ode_simple_pendulum,     equilibrium = c(0, 0),     parameters  = c(gL = 1, b = b)   )   result$b  <- b   result }))  # Parabola tau^2 = 4*delta (node/spiral boundary) tau_seq    <- seq(-3.2, 0.2, length.out = 200) parabola   <- data.frame(tau = tau_seq, delta = tau_seq^2 / 4)  ggplot(td_table, aes(x = tr, y = det)) +   # Parabola separating nodes from spirals   geom_line(data = parabola, aes(x = tau, y = delta),             color = "grey60", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +   # Reference lines   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.5) +   # Path through the plane as b increases   geom_path(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.6) +   geom_point(aes(color = classification), size = 3) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Type") +   # Annotate key regions   annotate("text", x = -2.5, y = 2.5,            label = "Stable
spiral",   size = 3, color = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = -2.5, y = 0.3,            label = "Stable
node",     size = 3, color = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x =  0.5, y = 2.5,            label = "Unstable
spiral", size = 3, color = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x = -1.0, y = -0.5,            label = "Saddle",           size = 3, color = "grey40") +   annotate("text", x =  0.1, y = 1.05,            label = "tau^2 == 4*Delta", parse = TRUE,            size  = 3, color = "grey50", hjust = 0) +   labs(     x     = "Trace (tau)",     y     = "Determinant (Delta)",     title = "Trace-determinant plane: pendulum origin as b increases"   ) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-3.5, 1), ylim = c(-0.8, 3)) +   theme_phase_plane()

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: saddle point, Jacobian one positive one negative eigenvalue. corresponding eigenvectors define two special trajectories: Stable manifold WsW^s: set initial conditions converge saddle t→+∞t \+\infty. Locally aligned eigenvector negative eigenvalue. Unstable manifold WuW^u: set initial conditions converge saddle t→−∞t \-\infty (equivalently, depart t→+∞t \+\infty). Locally aligned eigenvector positive eigenvalue. gg_manifolds() computes manifolds : Computing Jacobian eigenvectors saddle. Placing seed points distance epsilon saddle along eigenvector direction. Integrating forward unstable seeds backward stable seeds.

what: 6 Complete worked example: genetic toggle switch
title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: Gardner et al. (2000) genetic toggle switch models two mutually repressing proteins uu vv: dudt=α11+vβ−u,dvdt=α21+uγ−v.\frac{du}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_1}{1 + v^\beta} - u, \qquad \frac{dv}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_2}{1 + u^\gamma} - v. sufficiently large α1\alpha_1, α2\alpha_2 cooperativity coefficients β\beta, γ>1\gamma > 1, system bistable: two stable equilibria (one protein dominant) separated unstable saddle. bistability mechanism synthetic biological memory.
code: toggle_params <- c(alpha1 = 3, alpha2 = 3, beta = 2, gamma = 2)

previous_headings: 6 Complete worked example: genetic toggle switch
title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: toggle switch three equilibria: two stable nodes (one protein dominant) one saddle (unstable coexistence).
code: result_toggle <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_toggle,   xlim            = c(0, 4),   ylim            = c(0, 4),   parameters      = toggle_params,   t_end           = 10,   n_ic            = 5,   title           = "Genetic toggle switch",   legend_position = "bottom" ) result_toggle$plot +   labs(x = "Protein u", y = "Protein v") result_toggle$equilibria[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det")] #>          x        y classification tr        det #> 1 0.381966 2.618034    Stable node -2  0.5555555 #> 2 1.213412 1.213412         Saddle -2 -0.4186197 #> 3 2.618034 0.381966    Stable node -2  0.5555555

previous_headings: 6 Complete worked example: genetic toggle switch
title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: stable manifold (blue dashed curve) separatrix two basins attraction. perturbation pushes system across boundary causes irreversible switch one stable state — precisely “memory” property toggle switch.
code: # Extract the saddle saddle_toggle <- result_toggle$equilibria[   result_toggle$equilibria$classification == "Saddle", ] saddle_coords <- c(saddle_toggle$x[[1L]], saddle_toggle$y[[1L]])  # Reclassify to get the Jacobian cl_toggle <- classify_equilibrium(ode_toggle,                                    equilibrium = saddle_coords,                                    parameters  = toggle_params)  result_toggle$plot +   gg_manifolds(ode_toggle,                equilibrium   = saddle_coords,                parameters    = toggle_params,                eq_classified = cl_toggle,                t_manifold    = 6) +   labs(x     = "Protein u",        y     = "Protein v",        title = "Toggle switch: separatrix")

what: 7.1 Hopf bifurcation in the Van der Pol oscillator
title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: Van der Pol origin unstable spiral μ>0\mu > 0 center μ=0\mu = 0. μ\mu increases, trace Jacobian increases, moving origin unstable region. can track : μ=0\mu = 0 origin center (τ=0\tau = 0). μ>0\mu > 0 trace becomes positive origin unstable spiral — Hopf bifurcation occurs μ=0\mu = 0. μ>0\mu > 0 stable limit cycle exists (seen previous vignette).
code: mu_vals <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3)  hopf_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(mu_vals, function(mu) {   result    <- classify_equilibrium(     ode_van_der_pol,     equilibrium = c(0, 0),     parameters  = c(mu = mu)   )   result$mu <- mu   result }))  hopf_table[, c("mu", "classification", "tr", "det",                 "lambda_1_re", "lambda_1_im")] #>    mu  classification  tr det lambda_1_re lambda_1_im #> 1 0.0          Center 0.0   1    0.000000   1.0000000 #> 2 0.5 Unstable spiral 0.5   1    0.250000   0.9682458 #> 3 1.0 Unstable spiral 1.0   1    0.500000   0.8660254 #> 4 2.0   Unstable node 2.0   1    1.000000   0.0000000 #> 5 3.0   Unstable node 3.0   1    2.618034   0.0000000

title: Equilibrium Analysis
text: Consider user-defined 1D system bifurcation parameter rr: dydt=r+y2.\frac{dy}{dt} = r + y^2. r<0r < 0 two equilibria y*=±−ry^* = \pm\sqrt{-r}. r=0r = 0 collide saddle-node bifurcation disappear r>0r > 0.  three panels show phase line bifurcation (two equilibria), bifurcation (one semi-stable equilibrium), bifurcation (equilibria — solutions escape +∞+\infty).
code:
saddle_node <- function(y, parameters = NULL) {   r <- parameters[["r"]]   r + y^2 }  # r = -1: two equilibria eq_sn_neg <- find_equilibrium(saddle_node,                                y0 = NULL, system = "one.dim",                                ylim = c(-3, 3),                                parameters = c(r = -1)) cat("r = -1: equilibria at y =",     round(sapply(eq_sn_neg, `[[`, 1L), 4), "
") #> r = -1: equilibria at y = -1 1  # r = 0: one equilibrium (the bifurcation point) eq_sn_zero <- find_equilibrium(saddle_node,                                 y0 = NULL, system = "one.dim",                                 ylim = c(-1, 1),                                 parameters = c(r = 0)) cat("r = 0:  equilibria at y =",     round(sapply(eq_sn_zero, `[[`, 1L), 4), "
") #> r = 0:  equilibria at y = -1e-04 1e-04  # r = 1: no equilibria in this range eq_sn_pos <- find_equilibrium(saddle_node,                                y0 = NULL, system = "one.dim",                                ylim = c(-3, 3),                                parameters = c(r = 1)) #> Warning: No equilibria found. Try different starting points or a wider search #> range. cat("r = 1:  equilibria found:", length(eq_sn_pos), "
") #> r = 1:  equilibria found: 0 make_sn_plot <- function(r) {   gg_flow_field(     saddle_node,     xlim       = c(0, 3),     ylim       = c(-2.5, 2.5),     system     = "one.dim",     parameters = c(r = r),     title      = paste0("r = ", r)   ) +     gg_phase_portrait(       saddle_node,       ylim       = c(-2.5, 2.5),       xlim       = c(0, 3),       parameters = c(r = r)     ) }  if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(patchwork)   make_sn_plot(-1) | make_sn_plot(0) | make_sn_plot(1) } else {   make_sn_plot(-1) }

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: ggphasr provides tools qualitative analysis one- two-dimensional autonomous ordinary differential equation (ODE) systems using phase plane methods. modern successor phaseR package (Grayling, 2014), two key improvements: visualizations produced ggplot2 extensions, making every plot fully customizable standard ggplot2 syntax. package distributed via GitHub rather CRAN, ensuring remains installable regardless CRAN archival policies. ggphasr designed students instructors applied mathematics, mathematical modeling, differential equations, dynamical systems courses. Plots compose naturally using + operator — way ggplot2 plots built — students already familiar ggplot2 can immediately adapt extend phase portrait.

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: core workflow builds phase portrait layer layer using +, just like standard ggplot2:  function chain specific role: gg_flow_field() — creates base ggplot object direction arrows showing vector field (dx/dt,dy/dt)(dx/dt, dy/dt) grid point. returns complete ggplot object. gg_nullclines() — adds x-nullcline (dx/dt=0dx/dt = 0, red) y-nullcline (dy/dt=0dy/dt = 0, blue). Returns list ggplot2 layers. gg_trajectory() — numerically integrates ODE supplied initial conditions using deSolve adds solution paths. Returns list ggplot2 layers. gg_nullclines() gg_trajectory() return layer lists rather complete ggplot objects, compose naturally + just ggplot2 geom .
code: lv_params <- c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)  gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params,   xlab       = "Prey",   ylab       = "Predator",   title      = "Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_lotka_volterra,     xlim            = c(0, 5),     ylim            = c(0, 5),     parameters      = lv_params,     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_lotka_volterra,     y0         = matrix(c(0.5, 0.5,                           1.0, 3.0,                           3.5, 0.5,                           3.0, 3.5),                         ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE),     xlim       = c(0, 5),     ylim       = c(0, 5),     parameters = lv_params,     t_end      = 25,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   )

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: quick exploration, gg_phase_plane() produces complete phase portrait — flow field, nullclines, trajectories grid initial conditions, classified equilibria — single call:  gg_phase_plane() returns named list two components: $plot — ggplot object, can customized + $equilibria — tidy data frame classified equilibria add_layer() function adds ggplot2 layers result preserving $equilibria:
code: result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim            = c(0, 5),   ylim            = c(0, 5),   parameters      = lv_params,   legend_position = "bottom" )  result$plot result$equilibria[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det")] #>   x y classification tr det #> 1 0 0         Saddle  0  -1 #> 2 2 2         Center  0   1 add_layer(result, labs(title = "Lotka-Volterra: classified equilibria"))$plot

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: find_equilibrium() classify_equilibrium() provide numerical tools locating classifying equilibria:
code: # Find equilibria by grid search eq_list <- find_equilibrium(   ode_lotka_volterra,   y0         = NULL,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params )  # Classify each equilibrium do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_lotka_volterra,                        equilibrium = eq,                        parameters  = lv_params) }))[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det")] #>   x y classification tr det #> 1 0 0         Saddle  0  -1 #> 2 2 2         Center  0   1

title: Introduction to ggphasr

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: ggphasr includes 27 built-ODE systems ready use without writing equations: 1D growth models (4): ode_exponential(), ode_logistic(), ode_monomolecular(), ode_von_bertalanffy() Classical 2D models (8): ode_lotka_volterra(), ode_sir(), ode_van_der_pol(), ode_simple_pendulum(), ode_competition(), ode_toggle(), ode_morris_lecar(), ode_lindemann() Textbook examples (15): ode_example_01() ode_example_15() — library 1D 2D systems covering common equilibrium types (stable/unstable nodes, spirals, centers, saddles, limit cycles). built-systems use Convention can passed directly ggphasr plotting analysis function.

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: three topic vignettes provide full mathematical exposition detailed worked examples: Three vignettes provide detailed tutorials full mathematical exposition: One-Dimensional ODE Systems — phase lines, equilibria, stability, logistic von Bertalanffy growth models Two-Dimensional ODE Systems — phase planes, nullclines, trajectories, Lotka-Volterra, SIR, Van der Pol, competition Equilibrium Analysis — Jacobian linearization, trace-determinant plane, manifolds, bifurcation preview

title: Introduction to ggphasr
text: Grayling MJ (2014). phaseR: R Package Phase Plane Analysis Autonomous ODE Systems. R Journal 6(2): 43–51. https://doi.org/10.32614/RJ-2014-023 Strogatz SH (2015). Nonlinear Dynamics Chaos (2nd ed.). Westview Press.

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: one-dimensional autonomous ODE form dydt=f(y),\frac{dy}{dt} = f(y), derivative depends current state yy, time tt directly. Despite simplicity, 1D autonomous systems already exhibit rich qualitative behavior: exponential growth decay, logistic saturation, bistability, . Phase line analysis standard tool understanding systems without solving explicitly. key idea plot f(y)f(y) function yy, identify f(y)=0f(y) = 0 (equilibria), read direction flow sign f(y)f(y). vignette shows carry complete 1D phase analysis using ggphasr. cover: Defining 1D ODE systems ggphasr Plotting flow field phase line Adding nullclines trajectories Using find_equilibrium() classify_equilibrium() gg_phase_plane() --one wrapper Worked examples: logistic growth, user-defined cubic, von Bertalanffy growth model
code: library(ggphasr) library(ggplot2)

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: ggphasr accepts ODE functions two conventions. Convention (deSolve-compatible, recommended): First argument time t (present deSolve compatibility, ignored autonomous systems). Second argument y numeric vector state variables (length 1 1D systems). Third argument parameters named numeric vector model parameters. Return value list whose first element numeric vector derivatives. Convention B (simplified, quick exploration): time argument. Returns plain numeric scalar, list. ggphasr detects Convention B automatically wraps Convention internally. Note advanced users: Convention directly compatible deSolve::ode(), underpins numerical integration ggphasr. Convention B convenience wrapper; hood converted Convention computation. Throughout vignette use built-ode_logistic() define custom systems illustrate conventions.
code: f <- function(t, y, parameters) {   r <- parameters[["r"]]   K <- parameters[["K"]]   list(c(r * y[1] * (1 - y[1] / K))) } f <- function(y, parameters = NULL) {   r * y * (1 - y / K) }

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: logistic (Verhulst) model classic 1D ODE bounded population growth: dydt=ry(1−yK),\frac{dy}{dt} = r\,y\!\left(1 - \frac{y}{K}\right), y(t)y(t) population size, r>0r > 0 intrinsic growth rate, K>0K > 0 carrying capacity. model two equilibria: y*=0y^* = 0 (unstable — populations near zero grow away ) y*=Ky^* = K (stable — populations near KK return ). analytic solution familiar S-shaped logistic curve y(t)=K1+(K−y0y0)e−rt.y(t) = \frac{K}{1 + \left(\frac{K - y_0}{y_0}\right)e^{-rt}}.

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: gg_flow_field() system = "one.dim" plots direction field: arrows (t,y)(t, y) plane showing whether yy increasing decreasing point.  Notice arrows point upward (y increasing) 0<y<100 < y < 10 downward (y decreasing) y>10y > 10, reflecting two equilibria.
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_logistic,   xlim       = c(0, 8),   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),   xlab       = "Time t",   ylab       = "Population y",   title      = "Logistic growth: flow field" )

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: gg_phase_portrait() adds traditional phase line — vertical reference line directional arrows stability markers: Filled circle = stable equilibrium (arrows point toward ) Open circle = unstable equilibrium (arrows point away )
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_logistic,   xlim       = c(0, 8),   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),   xlab       = "Time t",   ylab       = "Population y",   title      = "Logistic growth: flow field + phase line" ) +   gg_phase_portrait(     ode_logistic,     ylim       = c(-1, 12),     xlim       = c(0, 8),     parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10)   )

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: nullcline 1D system simply set yy values f(y)=0f(y) = 0 — equilibrium points. gg_nullclines() draws horizontal lines. gg_trajectory() integrates ODE forward time specified initial conditions.  Four trajectories shown: two starting KK (growing toward ), one starting KK (declining toward ), one starting near zero. converge stable equilibrium y*=K=10y^* = K = 10.
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_logistic,   xlim       = c(0, 8),   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),   xlab       = "Time t",   ylab       = "Population y",   title      = "Logistic growth: complete phase portrait" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_logistic,     xlim            = c(0, 8),     ylim            = c(-1, 12),     system          = "one.dim",     parameters      = c(r = 1, K = 10),     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_logistic,     y0         = list(c(0.5), c(3), c(7), c(11.5)),     xlim       = c(0, 8),     ylim       = c(-1, 12),     system     = "one.dim",     parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),     t_end      = 7,     color      = "steelblue"   ) +   gg_phase_portrait(     ode_logistic,     ylim       = c(-1, 12),     xlim       = c(0, 8),     parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10)   )

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: gg_time_series() shows solutions plotted yy vs tt, familiar representation many courses:
code: gg_time_series(   ode_logistic,   y0         = list(c(0.5), c(3), c(7), c(11.5)),   t_end      = 8,   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),   xlab       = "Time t",   ylab       = "Population y(t)",   title      = "Logistic growth: time series" )

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: find_equilibrium() locates equilibria using Newton–Raphson root-finding. logistic equation know equilibria y*=0y^* = 0 y*=K=10y^* = K = 10, can verify:
code: eq_list <- find_equilibrium(   ode_logistic,   y0         = NULL,       # grid search over ylim   system     = "one.dim",   ylim       = c(-0.5, 11),   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10) ) eq_list #> [[1]] #> [1] -2.643387e-10 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 10

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: classify_equilibrium() computes f′(y*)f'(y^*) via finite differences uses sign determine stability: dfdy|y*<0⟹stable,dfdy|y*>0⟹unstable.\frac{df}{dy}\bigg|_{y^*} < 0 \implies \text{stable}, \qquad \frac{df}{dy}\bigg|_{y^*} > 0 \implies \text{unstable}. logistic equation, f(y)=ry(1−y/K)f(y) = ry(1 - y/K), f′(y)=r(1−2y/K)f'(y) = r(1 - 2y/K). y*=0y^* = 0: f′(0)=r>0f'(0) = r > 0 (unstable). y*=Ky^* = K: f′(K)=−r<0f'(K) = -r < 0 (stable).
code: eq_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_logistic,                        equilibrium = eq,                        system      = "one.dim",                        parameters  = c(r = 1, K = 10)) })) eq_table[, c("y", "classification", "jacobian_11")] #>               y classification jacobian_11 #> 1 -2.643387e-10       Unstable   0.9999999 #> 2  1.000000e+01         Stable  -1.0000001

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: illustrate working user-defined system, consider cubic: dydt=y(1−y)(2−y),\frac{dy}{dt} = y(1 - y)(2 - y), appears ode_example_02() ggphasr. system three equilibria y*=0y^* = 0, 11, 22 alternating stability (unstable, stable, unstable yy increases). can define Convention B conciseness: Convention B action: ggphasr detects first argument named y (t) automatically wraps function Convention passing deSolve.  expected: y*=0y^* = 0 unstable (f′(0)>0f'(0) > 0), y*=1y^* = 1 stable (f′(1)<0f'(1) < 0), y*=2y^* = 2 unstable (f′(2)>0f'(2) > 0).

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: von Bertalanffy model describes organism growth terms anabolism (proportional surface area ∼y2/3\sim y^{2/3}) catabolism (proportional mass ∼y\sim y): dydt=αy2/3−βy,\frac{dy}{dt} = \alpha\, y^{2/3} - \beta\, y, y(t)y(t) body mass, α>0\alpha > 0 anabolism coefficient, β>0\beta > 0 catabolism coefficient. Setting dy/dt=0dy/dt = 0 gives stable asymptotic mass y*=(αβ)3.y^* = \left(\frac{\alpha}{\beta}\right)^3. α=1\alpha = 1 β=0.5\beta = 0.5, get y*=(1/0.5)3=8y^* = (1/0.5)^3 = 8. Technical aside: y2/3y^{2/3} term means f(y)f(y) differentiable y=0y = 0, trivial equilibrium y=0y = 0 requires care. classify_equilibrium() return "Inconclusive (df/dy = 0)" finite-difference Jacobian undefined. expected biologically unimportant — organisms start positive mass.  dotted horizontal line marks analytic equilibrium y*=8y^* = 8. trajectories — regardless starting mass — converge , confirming y*y^* globally stable region y>0y > 0.
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_von_bertalanffy,   xlim       = c(0, 6),   ylim       = c(0, 14),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5),   xlab       = "Time t",   ylab       = "Body mass y",   title      = "Von Bertalanffy growth (alpha=1, beta=0.5)" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_von_bertalanffy,     xlim       = c(0, 6),     ylim       = c(0, 14),     system     = "one.dim",     parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5)   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_von_bertalanffy,     y0         = list(c(1), c(4), c(8), c(12)),     xlim       = c(0, 6),     ylim       = c(0, 14),     system     = "one.dim",     parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5),     t_end      = 5,     color      = "steelblue"   ) +   gg_phase_portrait(     ode_von_bertalanffy,     ylim       = c(0, 14),     xlim       = c(0, 6),     parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5)   ) +   # Mark the asymptotic mass y* = 8 analytically   geom_hline(yintercept = 8, linetype = "dotted", color = "firebrick") +   annotate("text", x = 5.5, y = 8.4,            label = "y* = 8", color = "firebrick", size = 3.5)

title: One-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: strength ggphasr’s composable design comparing systems side side requires swapping ODE function. compare time series three growth models carrying capacity K=10K = 10: Key differences: Exponential growth unbounded (carrying capacity). Logistic growth S-shaped curve inflection point y=K/2y = K/2. Monomolecular growth concave throughout — growth rate decreases monotonically without inflection point.
code: t_end  <- 10 params <- c(r = 1, K = 10) y0     <- list(c(1))  p_exp  <- gg_time_series(ode_exponential,   y0 = y0, t_end = t_end,                           system = "one.dim", parameters = c(r = 0.5),                           title = "Exponential")$plot +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 15))  p_log  <- gg_time_series(ode_logistic,       y0 = y0, t_end = t_end,                           system = "one.dim", parameters = params,                           title = "Logistic")$plot  p_mono <- gg_time_series(ode_monomolecular,  y0 = y0, t_end = t_end,                           system = "one.dim", parameters = params,                           title = "Monomolecular")$plot # Combine with patchwork if available, otherwise view separately if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(patchwork)   p_exp | p_log | p_mono } else {   p_log } #> logical(0)

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: two-dimensional autonomous ODE system form dxdt=f(x,y),dydt=g(x,y),\frac{dx}{dt} = f(x, y), \qquad \frac{dy}{dt} = g(x, y), derivatives depend current state (x,y)(x, y), time tt directly. Two-dimensional systems natural setting phase plane analysis: rather plotting state variable time, plot trajectories directly (x,y)(x, y) plane read qualitative behavior geometry. key objects 2D phase plane analysis : Flow field — arrows point (x,y)(x, y) showing direction (optionally) speed vector (f,g)(f, g). Nullclines — curves f(x,y)=0f(x, y) = 0 (x-nullcline) g(x,y)=0g(x, y) = 0 (y-nullcline). intersections equilibria. Trajectories — solution curves (x(t),y(t))(x(t), y(t)) specified initial conditions. Equilibria — points f=0f = 0 g=0g = 0 simultaneously, classified Jacobian eigenvalues. vignette covers: Defining 2D ODE systems ggphasr Building phase portrait layer layer Phase plane analysis Lotka–Volterra predator-prey model user-defined SIR epidemic model Van der Pol oscillator limit cycles two-species competition model bistability Time series plots
code: library(ggphasr) library(ggplot2)

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: 2D system, Convention requires state vector y length 2, y[1] = xx y[2] = yy, return value list containing length-2 derivative vector: Convention B 2D systems takes x y separate arguments returns plain numeric vector: ggphasr distinguishes two conventions checking whether first argument named t (Convention ) x (Convention B).
code: # Convention A (deSolve-compatible) my_ode <- function(t, y, parameters) {   x <- y[1]; v <- y[2]   list(c(     f(x, v),   # dx/dt     g(x, v)    # dy/dt   )) } # Convention B (simplified) my_ode <- function(x, y, parameters = NULL) {   c(f(x, y), g(x, y)) }

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: Lotka–Volterra equations classical model predator-prey dynamics: dxdt=αx−βxy,dydt=δxy−γy,\frac{dx}{dt} = \alpha x - \beta x y, \qquad \frac{dy}{dt} = \delta x y - \gamma y, x(t)x(t) prey abundance, y(t)y(t) predator abundance, α,β,δ,γ>0\alpha, \beta, \delta, \gamma > 0 rate constants. Equilibria: Trivial: (0,0)(0, 0) — species absent (unstable saddle) Interior: (γδ,αβ)\left(\dfrac{\gamma}{\delta},\, \dfrac{\alpha}{\beta}\right) — coexistence (neutrally stable center) Nullclines: x-nullcline (dx/dt=0dx/dt = 0): x=0x = 0 y=α/βy = \alpha/\beta y-nullcline (dy/dt=0dy/dt = 0): y=0y = 0 x=γ/δx = \gamma/\delta α=1\alpha = 1, β=0.5\beta = 0.5, δ=0.5\delta = 0.5, γ=1\gamma = 1, interior equilibrium (2,2)(2, 2).

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
code: lv_params <- c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)  gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params,   xlab       = "Prey x",   ylab       = "Predator y",   title      = "Lotka-Volterra: flow field" )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: x-nullcline (red, solid) y-nullcline (blue, dashed) intersect equilibria. visual intersection immediately reveals coexistence point (2,2)(2, 2).
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params,   xlab       = "Prey x",   ylab       = "Predator y",   title      = "Lotka-Volterra: flow field + nullclines" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_lotka_volterra,     xlim            = c(0, 5),     ylim            = c(0, 5),     parameters      = lv_params,     legend_position = "bottom"   )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: Trajectories show solutions orbit interior equilibrium indefinitely — signature neutrally stable center.
code: ics <- matrix(   c(0.5, 0.5,     1.0, 3.0,     3.5, 0.5,     3.0, 3.5),   ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE )  gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params,   xlab       = "Prey x",   ylab       = "Predator y",   title      = "Lotka-Volterra: complete phase portrait" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_lotka_volterra,     xlim            = c(0, 5),     ylim            = c(0, 5),     parameters      = lv_params,     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_lotka_volterra,     y0         = ics,     xlim       = c(0, 5),     ylim       = c(0, 5),     parameters = lv_params,     t_end      = 25,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: corresponding time series shows classic --phase oscillation prey predator:  Technical aside: Lotka–Volterra interior equilibrium center — special case linearization purely imaginary eigenvalues (λ=±iαγ\lambda = \pm \sqrt{\alpha\gamma}) Jacobian test inconclusive nonlinear stability. orbits fact closed curves (consequence conserved quantity), guaranteed linearization alone. Compare Van der Pol oscillator Section 5, stable limit cycle attracts nearby trajectories.
code: gg_time_series(   ode_lotka_volterra,   y0         = c(1, 1),   t_end      = 25,   parameters = lv_params,   var_labels = c("Prey x(t)", "Predator y(t)"),   title      = "Lotka-Volterra: time series" )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: SIR (Susceptible–Infected–Recovered) model describes epidemic dynamics closed population: dSdt=−βSI,dIdt=βSI−γI,\frac{dS}{dt} = -\beta S , \qquad \frac{dI}{dt} = \beta S - \gamma , S++R=1S + + R = 1 (proportions), β>0\beta > 0 transmission rate, γ>0\gamma > 0 recovery rate. basic reproduction number R0=β/γR_0 = \beta/\gamma determines whether epidemic occurs: epidemic grows R0S>1R_0 S > 1. define Convention B ODE illustrate simplified interface:  vertical dotted line marks S=1/R0=0.2S = 1/R_0 = 0.2. Trajectories cross x-nullcline (y-nullcline =0I = 0 S=γ/βS = \gamma/\beta) epidemic peak; afterward II declines. trajectories end x-axis (=0I = 0) — disease-free states — confirming epidemic always burns closed population. built-ode_sir() provides model additional scale parameter switching proportions absolute counts:
code: sir_ode <- function(x, y, parameters = NULL) {   # x = S (susceptible proportion)   # y = I (infected proportion)   beta  <- parameters[["beta"]]   gamma <- parameters[["gamma"]]   c(     -beta  * x * y,      beta  * x * y - gamma * y   ) }  sir_params <- c(beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1)   # R0 = 5 gg_flow_field(   sir_ode,   xlim       = c(0, 1),   ylim       = c(0, 0.3),   parameters = sir_params,   xlab       = "Susceptible S",   ylab       = "Infected I",   title      = "SIR model (R0 = 5): phase plane" ) +   gg_nullclines(     sir_ode,     xlim            = c(0, 1),     ylim            = c(0, 0.3),     parameters      = sir_params,     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     sir_ode,     y0         = list(c(0.99, 0.01), c(0.70, 0.01), c(0.30, 0.01)),     xlim       = c(0, 1),     ylim       = c(0, 0.3),     parameters = sir_params,     t_end      = 80,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   ) +   # Mark the epidemic threshold S = 1/R0 = gamma/beta   geom_vline(xintercept = sir_params["gamma"] / sir_params["beta"],              linetype = "dotted", color = "firebrick") +   annotate("text", x = 0.22, y = 0.28,            label = "S = 1/R0", color = "firebrick", size = 3.5,            hjust = 0) # Equivalent using the built-in system gg_flow_field(   ode_sir,   xlim       = c(0, 1),   ylim       = c(0, 0.3),   parameters = sir_params,   xlab       = "Susceptible S",   ylab       = "Infected I",   title      = "SIR model: built-in ode_sir()" ) +   gg_nullclines(ode_sir,                 xlim            = c(0, 1),                 ylim            = c(0, 0.3),                 parameters      = sir_params,                 legend_position = "bottom")

what: 4 The Van der Pol oscillator and limit cycles
title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: Van der Pol oscillator paradigmatic nonlinear oscillator stable limit cycle — closed trajectory attracts nearby solutions: dxdt=y,dydt=μ(1−x2)y−x,\frac{dx}{dt} = y, \qquad \frac{dy}{dt} = \mu(1 - x^2)y - x, μ≥0\mu \geq 0 controls strength nonlinear damping. μ=0\mu = 0 system reduces harmonic oscillator circular orbits. μ>0\mu > 0 unique stable limit cycle exists; origin unstable spiral.  trajectories — one starting inside limit cycle one outside — converge closed orbit. defining property stable limit cycle.
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_van_der_pol,   xlim       = c(-3, 3),   ylim       = c(-4, 4),   parameters = c(mu = 1),   title      = "Van der Pol (mu = 1): phase plane" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_van_der_pol,     xlim            = c(-3, 3),     ylim            = c(-4, 4),     parameters      = c(mu = 1),     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_van_der_pol,     y0         = list(c(0.1, 0.1),   # from inside                       c(2.5,  0.0),  # from outside                       c(-2.5, 0.0)),     xlim       = c(-3, 3),     ylim       = c(-4, 4),     parameters = c(mu = 1),     t_end      = 20,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   )

previous_headings: 4 The Van der Pol oscillator and limit cycles
what: 4.1 Effect of mu on the waveform
title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: μ\mu increases, limit cycle shape becomes increasingly non-circular, eventually producing sharp jumps characteristic relaxation oscillator:  Technical aside: existence uniqueness Van der Pol limit cycle follows Poincaré–Bendixson theorem combined Bendixson–Dulac criterion. key insight origin unstable spiral (tr J>0J > 0 origin) trajectories bounded, Poincaré–Bendixson must periodic orbit. Uniqueness follows specific structure nonlinearity.
code: make_vdp_ts <- function(mu) {   gg_time_series(     ode_van_der_pol,     y0         = c(0.1, 0.1),     t_end      = 30,     parameters = c(mu = mu),     var_labels = c("x", "y"),     title      = paste0("mu = ", mu)   ) }  if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(patchwork)   make_vdp_ts(0.5) | make_vdp_ts(1) | make_vdp_ts(3) } else {   make_vdp_ts(1) }

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: Lotka–Volterra competition model describes two species competing resource: dN1dt=r1N1(1−N1+α12N2K1),dN2dt=r2N2(1−N2+α21N1K2),\frac{dN_1}{dt} = r_1 N_1\!\left(1 - \frac{N_1 + \alpha_{12}N_2}{K_1}\right), \qquad \frac{dN_2}{dt} = r_2 N_2\!\left(1 - \frac{N_2 + \alpha_{21}N_1}{K_2}\right), α12\alpha_{12} per-capita effect species 2 species 1, vice versa. four possible qualitative outcomes depend relative magnitudes competition coefficients carrying capacities:

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: interspecific competition weaker intraspecific competition, stable coexistence equilibrium exists:
code: params_coexist <- c(r1=1, r2=1, K1=10, K2=10, a12=0.5, a21=0.5)  gg_flow_field(   ode_competition,   xlim       = c(0, 12),   ylim       = c(0, 12),   parameters = params_coexist,   xlab       = "Species 1 (N1)",   ylab       = "Species 2 (N2)",   title      = "Competition: stable coexistence" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_competition,     xlim            = c(0, 12),     ylim            = c(0, 12),     parameters      = params_coexist,     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_competition,     y0         = list(c(1,9), c(9,1), c(1,1), c(9,9)),     xlim       = c(0, 12),     ylim       = c(0, 12),     parameters = params_coexist,     t_end      = 20,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: interspecific competition stronger intraspecific competition, coexistence equilibrium becomes unstable saddle two stable single-species equilibria emerge. species wins depends entirely initial conditions — called priority effects founder control:  saddle point interior equilibrium divides phase plane two basins attraction. stable manifold separatrix — trajectories one side converge species 1 winning, side species 2 winning.
code: params_bistable <- c(r1=1, r2=1, K1=10, K2=10, a12=1.5, a21=1.5)  result_bistable <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_competition,   xlim            = c(0, 12),   ylim            = c(0, 12),   parameters      = params_bistable,   t_end           = 20,   n_ic            = 5,   title           = "Competition: bistability (priority effects)",   legend_position = "bottom" ) result_bistable$plot +   labs(x = "Species 1 (N1)", y = "Species 2 (N2)") result_bistable$equilibria[, c("x", "y", "classification")] #>               x  y classification #> 1 -1.426918e-11 10    Stable node #> 2  0.000000e+00  0  Unstable node #> 3  4.000000e+00  4         Saddle #> 4  1.000000e+01  0    Stable node

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: blue dashed curves (stable manifold) form separatrix. Trajectories starting converge species 2 winning; converge species 1 winning.
code: # Extract the saddle from the equilibrium table saddle_row <- result_bistable$equilibria[   result_bistable$equilibria$classification == "Saddle", ] saddle_eq  <- c(saddle_row$x[[1L]], saddle_row$y[[1L]])  result_bistable$plot +   gg_manifolds(     ode_competition,     equilibrium = saddle_eq,     parameters  = params_bistable,     t_manifold  = 8   ) +   labs(x     = "Species 1 (N1)",        y     = "Species 2 (N2)",        title = "Competition: bistability with separatrix")

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: simple pendulum illustrates 2D phase plane can represent second-order ODE. Writing θ\theta angle vertical ω=dθ/dt\omega = d\theta/dt angular velocity: dθdt=ω,dωdt=−gLsin⁡θ−bω,\frac{d\theta}{dt} = \omega, \qquad \frac{d\omega}{dt} = -\frac{g}{L}\sin\theta - b\,\omega, g/Lg/L ratio gravitational acceleration pendulum length b≥0b \geq 0 damping coefficient. equilibria (θ,ω)=(kπ,0)(\theta, \omega) = (k\pi, 0) integer kk. Even multiples π\pi stable (pendulum hanging ); odd multiples unstable saddles (pendulum balanced upright).   damping, stable equilibria θ=0\theta = 0 become stable spirals (small bb) stable nodes (large bb), heteroclinic orbits connecting saddles ±π\pm\pi break spiraling trajectories.
code: gg_flow_field(   ode_simple_pendulum,   xlim       = c(-pi, pi),   ylim       = c(-3, 3),   parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0),   n_points   = 25,   xlab       = "Angle theta (radians)",   ylab       = "Angular velocity omega",   title      = "Simple pendulum: undamped (b = 0)" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_simple_pendulum,     xlim            = c(-pi, pi),     ylim            = c(-3, 3),     parameters      = c(gL = 1, b = 0),     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_simple_pendulum,     y0         = list(c(0.5, 0), c(1.5, 0), c(2.5, 0)),     xlim       = c(-pi, pi),     ylim       = c(-3, 3),     parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0),     t_end      = 12,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),     labels = c("-pi", "-pi/2", "0", "pi/2", "pi")   ) gg_flow_field(   ode_simple_pendulum,   xlim       = c(-pi, pi),   ylim       = c(-3, 3),   parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0.5),   n_points   = 25,   xlab       = "Angle theta (radians)",   ylab       = "Angular velocity omega",   title      = "Simple pendulum: damped (b = 0.5)" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_simple_pendulum,     xlim            = c(-pi, pi),     ylim            = c(-3, 3),     parameters      = c(gL = 1, b = 0.5),     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_simple_pendulum,     y0         = list(c(0.5, 0), c(1.5, 0), c(2.5, 0)),     xlim       = c(-pi, pi),     ylim       = c(-3, 3),     parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0.5),     t_end      = 15,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),     labels = c("-pi", "-pi/2", "0", "pi/2", "pi")   )

title: Two-Dimensional ODE Systems
text: gg_* functions return standard ggplot2 objects layer lists, full ggplot2 customization available. demonstrate several common customizations Lotka–Volterra system:
code: # When color_by_magnitude = TRUE, gg_flow_field() uses a continuous color # scale for arrow magnitude. To avoid a scale conflict, gg_nullclines() # must use add_legend = FALSE so it draws fixed-color lines without # adding a competing discrete color scale. gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim               = c(0, 5),   ylim               = c(0, 5),   parameters         = lv_params,   arrow_type         = "proportional",   color_by_magnitude = TRUE,   magnitude_palette  = c("grey90", "#2c7bb6"),   n_points           = 25 ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_lotka_volterra,     xlim       = c(0, 5),     ylim       = c(0, 5),     parameters = lv_params,     x_color    = "#d73027",     y_color    = "#1a9641",     linewidth  = 1.2,     add_legend = FALSE       # avoids discrete/continuous scale conflict   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_lotka_volterra,     y0         = list(c(1, 0.5), c(3.5, 0.5), c(0.5, 3)),     xlim       = c(0, 5),     ylim       = c(0, 5),     parameters = lv_params,     t_end      = 25,     color      = "black",     linewidth  = 0.9,     add_start_point = FALSE   ) +   geom_point(     data    = data.frame(x = 2, y = 2),     mapping = aes(x = x, y = y),     shape = 21, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 4, stroke = 1.2   ) +   labs(     x     = "Prey x(t)",     y     = "Predator y(t)",     title = "Lotka-Volterra: customized phase portrait",     color = "Speed"   )

what: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: ggphasr provides tools qualitative analysis one- two-dimensional autonomous ordinary differential equation (ODE) systems using phase plane methods. visualizations produced ggplot2 extensions, making every plot fully customizable standard ggplot2 syntax. ggphasr modern successor phaseR package (Grayling, 2014), base R graphics replaced ggplot2.

title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jmgraham30/ggphasr")

title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: ggphasr functions compose naturally + operator, just like ggplot2 . complete phase portrait built layer layer:  quick exploration, gg_phase_plane() produces complete analysis — flow field, nullclines, trajectories, classified equilibria — single call:
code: library(ggphasr) library(ggplot2)  lv_params <- c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)  gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = lv_params,   xlab       = "Prey",   ylab       = "Predator",   title      = "Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model" ) +   gg_nullclines(     ode_lotka_volterra,     xlim            = c(0, 5),     ylim            = c(0, 5),     parameters      = lv_params,     legend_position = "bottom"   ) +   gg_trajectory(     ode_lotka_volterra,     y0         = matrix(c(0.5, 0.5,                           1.0, 3.0,                           3.5, 0.5,                           3.0, 3.5),                         ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE),     xlim       = c(0, 5),     ylim       = c(0, 5),     parameters = lv_params,     t_end      = 25,     color      = "grey30",     add_start_point = FALSE   ) result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim            = c(0, 5),   ylim            = c(0, 5),   parameters      = lv_params,   legend_position = "bottom" )  result$plot result$equilibria[, c("x", "y", "classification")] #>   x y classification #> 1 0 0         Saddle #> 2 2 2         Center

title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: ggphasr accepts ODE functions two calling conventions. Convention — deSolve-compatible (recommended compatibility deSolve::ode()): Convention B — simplified, quick exploration: convention detected automatically function’s argument names. built-ODE systems use Convention .
code: my_ode <- function(t, y, parameters) {   list(c(dy1, dy2)) } my_ode <- function(x, y, parameters = NULL) {   c(dx, dy) }

title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: 1D models: ode_exponential(), ode_logistic(), ode_monomolecular(), ode_von_bertalanffy() 2D models: ode_lotka_volterra(), ode_sir(), ode_van_der_pol(), ode_simple_pendulum(), ode_competition(), ode_toggle(), ode_morris_lecar(), ode_lindemann() Textbook examples: ode_example_01() ode_example_15()

title: Phase Plane Analysis of ODE Systems Using ggplot2
text: find_equilibrium() classify_equilibrium() provide complete numerical workflow locating classifying equilibria:
code: # Find all equilibria of the competition model by grid search eq_list <- find_equilibrium(   ode_competition,   y0         = NULL,   xlim       = c(0, 12),   ylim       = c(0, 12),   parameters = c(r1=1, r2=1, K1=10, K2=10, a12=0.5, a21=0.5) )  # Classify each equilibrium do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_competition, equilibrium = eq,                        parameters = c(r1=1, r2=1, K1=10, K2=10,                                       a12=0.5, a21=0.5)) }))[, c("x", "y", "classification", "tr", "det")] #>               x         y classification         tr        det #> 1 -4.425602e-10 10.000000         Saddle -0.5000002 -0.4999999 #> 2  0.000000e+00  0.000000  Unstable node  1.9999998  0.9999998 #> 3  6.666667e+00  6.666667    Stable node -1.3333335  0.3333335 #> 4  1.000000e+01  0.000000         Saddle -0.5000002 -0.4999999

what: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
text: Computes Jacobian matrix one- two-dimensional ODE system known equilibrium point using forward finite differences, classifies equilibrium using eigenvalue analysis. Returns tidy data frame row can combined results multiple equilibria using rbind().

title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
code: classify_equilibrium(   deriv,   equilibrium,   system = c("two.dim", "one.dim"),   parameters = NULL,   h = 1e-06,   tol = 1e-08 )

title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
text: deriv function describing ODE system, Convention B. equilibrium Numeric vector length 1 (1D) 2 (2D) giving equilibrium coordinates. Typically obtained find_equilibrium(). system Character: "two.dim" (default) "one.dim". parameters Parameter vector list passed deriv. h Numeric. Step size finite-difference Jacobian computation. Default: 1e-6. tol Numeric. Tolerance treating eigenvalue real/imaginary parts zero. Default: 1e-8.

title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
text: data.frame() one row following columns: x x-coordinate equilibrium (2D) NA (1D). y y-coordinate equilibrium (1D 2D). classification Character. One : "Stable node", "Unstable node", "Stable spiral", "Unstable spiral", "Center", "Saddle", "Non-isolated equilibrium" (2D); "Stable", "Unstable", "Inconclusive (df/dy = 0)" (1D). tr Trace Jacobian (2D ; NA 1D). det Determinant Jacobian (2D ; NA 1D). jacobian_11 J[1,1] — always present. jacobian_12 J[1,2] (2D ; NA 1D). jacobian_21 J[2,1] (2D ; NA 1D). jacobian_22 J[2,2] (2D ; NA 1D). lambda_1_re Real part eigenvalue 1 (2D ; NA 1D). lambda_1_im Imaginary part eigenvalue 1 (2D ). lambda_2_re Real part eigenvalue 2 (2D ). lambda_2_im Imaginary part eigenvalue 2 (2D ).

title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
text: Results multiple equilibria can combined summary table:
code: eq_list <- find_equilibrium(ode_example_11, y0 = NULL,                              xlim = c(0,4), ylim = c(0,4)) results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_example_11, equilibrium = eq) }))

title: Classify an equilibrium point of an ODE system — classify_equilibrium
code: # Classify the interior equilibrium of Lotka-Volterra (expect: Center) classify_equilibrium(   ode_lotka_volterra,   equilibrium = c(2, 2),   parameters  = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1) ) #>   x y classification tr det jacobian_11 jacobian_12 jacobian_21 jacobian_22 #> 1 2 2         Center  0   1           0          -1           1           0 #>   lambda_1_re lambda_1_im lambda_2_re lambda_2_im #> 1           0           1           0          -1  # Classify all equilibria of example 11 eq_list <- find_equilibrium(ode_example_11, y0 = NULL,                              xlim = c(0, 4), ylim = c(0, 4)) do.call(rbind, lapply(eq_list, function(eq) {   classify_equilibrium(ode_example_11, equilibrium = eq) })) #>               x y classification        tr       det jacobian_11   jacobian_12 #> 1 -9.524185e-10 2    Stable node -3.000002  2.000003   -1.000001  1.904837e-09 #> 2  0.000000e+00 0  Unstable node  4.999998  5.999995    2.999999  0.000000e+00 #> 3  1.000000e+00 1         Saddle -2.000002 -0.999998   -1.000001 -2.000000e+00 #> 4  3.000000e+00 0    Stable node -4.000002  3.000004   -3.000001 -6.000000e+00 #>   jacobian_21 jacobian_22 lambda_1_re lambda_1_im lambda_2_re lambda_2_im #> 1          -2   -2.000001   -2.000001           0  -1.0000010           0 #> 2           0    1.999999    2.999999           0   1.9999990           0 #> 3          -1   -1.000001   -2.414215           0   0.4142126           0 #> 4           0   -1.000001   -3.000001           0  -1.0000010           0

what: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
text: Locates one equilibrium points one- two-dimensional autonomous ODE system using Newton-Raphson root-finding via rootSolve::multiroot(). Accepts either single initial guess grid starting points search multiple equilibria.

title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
code: find_equilibrium(   deriv,   y0 = NULL,   system = c("two.dim", "one.dim"),   parameters = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   n_grid = 10L,   tol = 1e-08,   dedup_tol = 1e-04 )

title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
text: deriv function describing ODE system, Convention B. See ggphasr-package details. y0 Initial guess(es) root-finder. One : numeric vector length 1 (1D) 2 (2D): single starting point. numeric matrix one row per starting point. list numeric vectors. NULL: triggers automatic grid search xlim × ylim (requires xlim ylim supplied). system Character: "two.dim" (default) "one.dim". parameters Parameter vector list passed deriv. xlim Numeric vector length 2. x-axis search range. Required y0 = NULL. Default: NULL. ylim Numeric vector length 2. y-axis search range. Required y0 = NULL. Default: NULL. n_grid Integer. Grid resolution per axis automatic search y0 = NULL. Default: 10 (giving 100 starting points 2D systems). tol Numeric. Convergence tolerance passed rootSolve::multiroot(). Default: 1e-8. dedup_tol Numeric. Distance tolerance removing duplicate roots. Two equilibria closer treated point. Default: 1e-4.

title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
text: list numeric vectors, giving coordinates one equilibrium point. one equilibrium found, list length 1 returned (unwrapped vector) consistency.

title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
text: Newton-Raphson sensitive initial guess — may fail converge converge non-equilibrium point badly chosen starting values. y0 = NULL, function tries n_grid^2 starting points regular grid, collects converged roots, deduplicates . reliable single guess still exhaustive. Failed convergences silently discarded; roots residual tol * 100 retained.

title: Find equilibria of an ODE system numerically — find_equilibrium
code: # Single guess: find the interior equilibrium of Lotka-Volterra find_equilibrium(   ode_lotka_volterra,   y0         = c(1.5, 1.5),   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1) ) #> [[1]] #> [1] 2 2 #>   # Automatic grid search: find all equilibria of example 11 find_equilibrium(   ode_example_11,   y0    = NULL,   xlim  = c(0, 4),   ylim  = c(0, 4) ) #> [[1]] #> [1] -9.524185e-10  2.000000e+00 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 0 0 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] 1 1 #>  #> [[4]] #> [1] 3 0 #>   # 1D system: find equilibria of the logistic equation find_equilibrium(   ode_logistic,   y0         = NULL,   system     = "one.dim",   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10) ) #> [[1]] #> [1] -1.709727e-16 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 10 #>

what: Plot a flow field (direction field) for an ODE system — gg_flow_field
title: Plot a flow field (direction field) for an ODE system — gg_flow_field
text: Computes plots direction velocity field one- two-dimensional autonomous ODE system regular grid. Returns complete ggplot2::ggplot() object additional layers (nullclines, trajectories, etc.) can added +.

title: Plot a flow field (direction field) for an ODE system — gg_flow_field
code: gg_flow_field(   deriv,   xlim,   ylim,   system = c("two.dim", "one.dim"),   parameters = NULL,   n_points = 21L,   arrow_type = c("equal", "proportional"),   arrow_color = "grey60",   arrow_size = 0.25,   arrow_linewidth = 0.4,   arrow_length_scale = 0.85,   max_magnitude = NULL,   color_by_magnitude = FALSE,   magnitude_palette = c("grey80", "#2c7bb6"),   add_origin_lines = TRUE,   origin_line_color = "grey40",   legend_position = "right",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = "y",   title = NULL )

title: Plot a flow field (direction field) for an ODE system — gg_flow_field
text: deriv function (named list functions) describing ODE system, either Convention (f(t, y, parameters) returning list(c(...))) Convention B (simplified f(x, y, parameters) returning c(...)). See ggphasr-package details ODE conventions. overlay multiple systems, pass named list: deriv = list(system1 = f1, system2 = f2). xlim Numeric vector length 2. Range x-axis (time axis 1D systems). Required. ylim Numeric vector length 2. Range y-axis (state variable axis). Required. system Character string: "two.dim" (default) "one.dim". parameters numeric vector list parameter values passed deriv. deriv list functions, parameters can named list parameter vectors, one per system; single vector applied systems. n_points Integer. Number grid points along axis. Default: 21 (matching phaseR's default). arrow_type Character string: "equal" (default, arrows length, showing direction ) "proportional" (arrow length proportional vector magnitude). arrow_color Character. Color arrows. Default: "grey60". Ignored color_by_magnitude = TRUE. arrow_size Numeric. Relative size arrow heads. Default: 0.25. arrow_linewidth Numeric. Line width arrow shafts. Default: 0.4. arrow_length_scale Numeric (0, 1]. Maximum arrow length fraction grid cell size. Default: 0.85. max_magnitude Numeric NULL. arrow_type = "proportional" : supplied, arrow lengths scaled relative reference magnitude rather maximum grid. Useful producing consistent scaling across multiple plots. Default: NULL (auto-scale grid maximum). color_by_magnitude Logical. TRUE, arrows colored vector magnitude using continuous color scale. Overrides arrow_color. Default: FALSE. magnitude_palette Character vector length 2. Low high colors magnitude color scale, used color_by_magnitude = TRUE. Default: c("grey80", "#2c7bb6"). add_origin_lines Logical. TRUE (default), adds thin reference lines x = 0 y = 0 (y = 0 1D systems) via ggplot2::geom_hline() ggplot2::geom_vline(). origin_line_color Character. Color origin reference lines. Default: "grey40". legend_position Character string numeric vector length 2. Controls legend placement color_by_magnitude = TRUE multiple systems supplied via list. One "right" (default), "left", "top", "bottom", "none", "inside", numeric vector c(x, y) values [0, 1] custom inside position. xlab Character. x-axis label. Default: "x" 2D systems, "t" 1D systems. ylab Character. y-axis label. Default: "y". title Character NULL. Plot title. Default: NULL.

title: Plot a flow field (direction field) for an ODE system — gg_flow_field
code: # 2D system: Lotka-Volterra phase plane gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1) )   # 1D system: logistic growth phase portrait gg_flow_field(   ode_logistic,   xlim       = c(0, 4),   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10) )   # Proportional arrows colored by magnitude gg_flow_field(   ode_van_der_pol,   xlim                = c(-3, 3),   ylim                = c(-3, 3),   parameters          = c(mu = 1),   arrow_type          = "proportional",   color_by_magnitude  = TRUE )   # Compose with nullclines and a custom title gg_flow_field(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1),   title      = "Lotka-Volterra" ) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Prey", y = "Predator")

what: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
title: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
text: Computes draws stable /unstable manifolds saddle point two-dimensional autonomous ODE system. Returns list ggplot2 layer objects can added phase plane plot +.

title: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
code: gg_manifolds(   deriv,   equilibrium,   parameters = NULL,   eq_classified = NULL,   draw_stable = TRUE,   draw_unstable = TRUE,   t_manifold = 10,   t_steps = 500L,   epsilon = 1e-04,   method = "lsoda",   stable_color = "#4575b4",   unstable_color = "#d73027",   linewidth = 1,   stable_linetype = "dashed",   unstable_linetype = "solid",   add_arrows = TRUE,   arrow_size = 0.35,   add_legend = FALSE,   h = 1e-06 )

title: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
text: deriv function describing 2D ODE system, Convention B. See ggphasr details. equilibrium Numeric vector length 2 giving saddle point coordinates. Typically obtained find_equilibrium(). parameters Parameter vector list passed deriv. eq_classified data frame returned classify_equilibrium(), NULL (default). supplied, Jacobian read object rather recomputed. draw_stable Logical. Whether draw stable manifold. Default: TRUE. draw_unstable Logical. Whether draw unstable manifold. Default: TRUE. t_manifold Numeric. Integration time manifold branch. Default: 10. t_steps Integer. Number integration steps per branch. Default: 500. epsilon Numeric. Distance saddle seed points. Default: 1e-4. method Character. deSolve integration method. Default: "lsoda". stable_color Character. Color stable manifold. Default: "#4575b4" (blue). unstable_color Character. Color unstable manifold. Default: "#d73027" (red). linewidth Numeric. Line width manifolds. Default: 1. stable_linetype Character integer. Line type stable manifold. Default: "dashed". unstable_linetype Character integer. Line type unstable manifold. Default: "solid". add_arrows Logical. Whether add direction arrows. Default: TRUE. arrow_size Numeric. Arrow head size lines. Default: 0.35. add_legend Logical. Whether add legend. Default: FALSE. Note: setting TRUE adds scale_color_manual() scale_linetype_manual() layers, conflict parent plot already color linetype scales. h Numeric. Finite-difference step Jacobian. Default: 1e-6.

title: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
text: list ggplot2 layer objects. Add ggplot +. Returns empty list (invisibly) warning equilibrium saddle point.

title: Draw stable and unstable manifolds of a saddle point — gg_manifolds
text: manifolds computed : Finding eigenvectors Jacobian saddle point. Placing seed points distance epsilon saddle along eigenvector (+ - directions, giving four seeds total). Integrating forward two unstable seeds trace unstable manifold. Integrating backward two stable seeds trace stable manifold. classify_equilibrium() result supplied via eq_classified, Jacobian entries reused avoid redundant computation. Otherwise Jacobian computed internally via finite differences.

title: Complete phase plane analysis in a single call — gg_phase_plane

title: Complete phase plane analysis in a single call — gg_phase_plane
text: deriv function describing ODE system, Convention B. See ggphasr-package details. xlim Numeric vector length 2. x-axis range. Required. ylim Numeric vector length 2. y-axis range. Required. system Character: "two.dim" (default) "one.dim". parameters Parameter vector list passed deriv. n_points Integer. Flow field grid resolution. Default: 21. arrow_type Character: "equal" (default) "proportional". arrow_color Character. Flow field arrow color. Default: "grey70". show_nullclines Logical. Whether draw nullclines. Default: TRUE. nullcline_n_points Integer. Nullcline grid resolution. Default: 250. y0 Initial condition(s) trajectories. numeric vector, matrix, list accepted gg_trajectory(). NULL (default), regular grid n_ic x n_ic initial conditions used automatically. show_trajectories Logical. Whether draw trajectories. Default: TRUE. n_ic Integer. Number auto-generated initial conditions per axis (ignored y0 supplied). Default: 4 (giving 16 ICs 2D systems, 4 1D). t_end Numeric. Forward integration time. Default: 10. t_start_back Numeric NULL. Backward integration time. Default: NULL. trajectory_color Character NULL. NULL (default) multiple ICs used, trajectory gets distinct color. color string, trajectories share color. find_equilibria Logical. Whether automatically find, classify, annotate equilibria. Default: TRUE. Uses grid search xlim x ylim. eq_n_grid Integer. Grid resolution equilibrium search. Default: 10. eq_grid_y0 List NULL. Custom starting points equilibrium search. NULL, regular grid used. legend_position Character string numeric vector length 2. Controls position legends (equilibrium types, nullclines) plot. title: Complete phase plane analysis in a single call — gg_phase_plane
code: # Minimal call: produces everything automatically result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1) ) result$plot  result$equilibria[, c("x", "y", "classification")] #>   x y classification #> 1 0 0         Saddle #> 2 2 2         Center  # 1D system result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_logistic,   xlim       = c(0, 8),   ylim       = c(-1, 12),   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10) ) result$plot   # Suppress equilibrium search for speed result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_van_der_pol,   xlim            = c(-3, 3),   ylim            = c(-4, 4),   parameters      = c(mu = 1),   find_equilibria = FALSE,   t_end           = 20 ) result$plot   # Supply custom initial conditions ics <- matrix(c(0.5,0.5, 1,2, 3,1, 2,3), ncol=2, byrow=TRUE) result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_lotka_volterra,   xlim       = c(0, 5),   ylim       = c(0, 5),   parameters = c(alpha=1, beta=0.5, delta=0.5, gamma=1),   y0         = ics ) result$plot   # Further customize the returned plot result <- gg_phase_plane(   ode_competition,   xlim       = c(0, 12),   ylim       = c(0, 12),   parameters = c(r1=1, r2=1, K1=10, K2=10, a12=0.5, a21=0.5) ) result$plot +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Species 1", y = "Species 2",                 title = "Competition model")

Default: "white". eq_size Numeric. Size equilibrium points. Default: 4. eq_stroke Numeric. Border width equilibrium points. Default: 1. line_color Character. Color vertical phase line. title: Plot time series of ODE solutions — gg_time_series
code: # 1D time series: logistic growth from several initial conditions gg_time_series(   ode_logistic,   y0         = list(c(0.5), c(3), c(7), c(12)),   t_end      = 8,   system     = "one.dim",   parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10),   title      = "Logistic growth" )   # 2D time series: Lotka-Volterra — prey and predator in separate panels gg_time_series(   ode_lotka_volterra,   y0         = list(c(1, 1), c(3, 2)),   t_end      = 20,   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1),   var_labels = c("Prey", "Predator"),   title      = "Lotka-Volterra time series" )   # Combine with phase portrait to see the same trajectory two ways lv_params <- c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)  # Phase plane view p_phase <- gg_flow_field(ode_lotka_volterra,                           xlim = c(0,5), ylim = c(0,5),                           parameters = lv_params) +   gg_trajectory(ode_lotka_volterra, y0 = c(1, 1),                 xlim = c(0,5), ylim = c(0,5),                 parameters = lv_params, color = "black")  # Time domain view p_time <- gg_time_series(ode_lotka_volterra, y0 = c(1, 1),                           t_end = 20, parameters = lv_params)

code: # Single trajectory on a Lotka-Volterra phase plane lv_params <- c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)  gg_flow_field(ode_lotka_volterra,               xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5),               parameters = lv_params) +   gg_trajectory(ode_lotka_volterra,                 y0         = c(1, 1),                 xlim       = c(0, 5),                 ylim       = c(0, 5),                 parameters = lv_params)   # Multiple initial conditions from a matrix ics <- matrix(c(0.5, 0.5,                 1.0, 2.0,                 3.0, 1.0), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)  gg_flow_field(ode_lotka_volterra,               xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5),               parameters = lv_params) +   gg_nullclines(ode_lotka_volterra,                 xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5),                 parameters = lv_params) +   gg_trajectory(ode_lotka_volterra,                 y0         = ics,                 xlim       = c(0, 5),                 ylim       = c(0, 5),                 parameters = lv_params,                 t_end      = 20) #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.   # Forward and backward integration near a saddle point gg_flow_field(ode_example_08,               xlim = c(-3, 3), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +   gg_trajectory(ode_example_08,                 y0           = c(0.1, 2),                 xlim         = c(-3, 3),                 ylim         = c(-3, 3),                 t_end        = 3,                 t_start_back = -3)

title: Two-species Lotka-Volterra competition model — ode_competition
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = \(N_1\), \(y[2]\) = \(N_2\). parameters Named numeric vector elements: r1 Growth rate species 1. Default: 1. r2 Growth rate species 2. Default: 1. K1 Carrying capacity species 1. Default: 10. K2 Carrying capacity species 2. Default: 10. a12 Effect species 2 species 1 (\(\alpha_{12}\)). Default: 0.5. a21 Effect species 1 species 2 (\(\alpha_{21}\)). Default: 0.5.

title: Two-species Lotka-Volterra competition model — ode_competition
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((dN_1/dt, dN_2/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Two-species Lotka-Volterra competition model — ode_competition
text: $$\frac{dN_1}{dt} = r_1 N_1 \left(1 - \frac{N_1 + \alpha_{12} N_2}{K_1}\right)$$ $$\frac{dN_2}{dt} = r_2 N_2 \left(1 - \frac{N_2 + \alpha_{21} N_1}{K_2}\right)$$ \(N_1, N_2\) species abundances, \(r_1, r_2\) intrinsic growth rates, \(K_1, K_2\) carrying capacities, \(\alpha_{12}, \alpha_{21}\) interspecific competition coefficients (effect species 2 species 1, vice versa). four possible outcomes (competitive exclusion species 1 2, stable coexistence, unstable equilibrium priority effects) depend relative magnitudes \(K_1\), \(K_2\), \(\alpha_{12}\), \(\alpha_{21}\), making excellent teaching example phase plane analysis.

title: Two-species Lotka-Volterra competition model — ode_competition
code: ode_competition(t = 0, y = c(5, 5),                parameters = c(r1 = 1, r2 = 1,                               K1 = 10, K2 = 10,                               a12 = 0.5, a21 = 0.5)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1.25 1.25 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_competition, xlim = c(0, 15), ylim = c(0, 15)) } # }

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
text: ODE exponential (Malthusian) population growth:

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
code: ode_exponential(t, y, parameters = c(r = 0.5))

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (used directly, included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 1. Current state: \(y[1]\) population size. parameters Named numeric vector element: r Intrinsic growth rate. Default: 0.5.

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
text: list one element: numeric vector length 1 containing \(dy/dt\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
text: $$\frac{dy}{dt} = r \, y$$ \(y(t)\) population size (quantity growing proportionally ) \(r\) intrinsic growth rate. \(r > 0\) population grows without bound; \(r < 0\) decays zero; \(r = 0\) constant. analytic solution \(y(t) = y_0 \, e^{rt}\).

title: Exponential growth model — ode_exponential
code: # Evaluate at y = 2 with default parameters (r = 0.5) # dy/dt = 0.5 * 2 = 1 ode_exponential(t = 0, y = c(2), parameters = c(r = 0.5)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>   # Use with gg_flow_field() for a 1D phase portrait if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_phase_portrait(ode_exponential, ylim = c(-3, 3)) } # }
