text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((d[]/dt, d[^*]/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Lindemann mechanism (chemical kinetics) — ode_lindemann
text: Reaction scheme: $$+ \xrightarrow{k_1} ^* + \quad \text{(activation)}$$ $$^* + \xrightarrow{k_{-1}} + \quad \text{(deactivation)}$$ $$^* \xrightarrow{k_2} P \quad \text{(product formation)}$$ resulting ODE system \([]\) \([^*]\) : $$\frac{d[]}{dt}   = -k_1 []^2 + k_{-1} [^*][]$$ $$\frac{d[^*]}{dt} =  k_1 []^2 - k_{-1} [^*][] - k_2 [^*]$$

title: Lindemann mechanism (chemical kinetics) — ode_lindemann
code: ode_lindemann(t = 0, y = c(2, 0.1),              parameters = c(k1 = 1, k_1 = 1, k2 = 0.5)) #> [[1]] #> [1] -3.80  3.75 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_lindemann, xlim = c(0, 3), ylim = c(0, 1)) } # }

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
text: ODE logistic (Verhulst) population growth:

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
code: ode_logistic(t, y, parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10))

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (used directly, included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 1. Current state: \(y[1]\) population size. parameters Named numeric vector elements: r Intrinsic growth rate. Default: 1. K Carrying capacity. Default: 10.

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
text: list one element: numeric vector length 1 containing \(dy/dt\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
text: $$\frac{dy}{dt} = r \, y \left(1 - \frac{y}{K}\right)$$ \(y(t)\) population size, \(r > 0\) intrinsic growth rate, \(K > 0\) carrying capacity. model two equilibria: unstable equilibrium \(y = 0\) stable equilibrium \(y = K\). analytic solution \(y(t) = K / (1 + ((K - y_0)/y_0) \, e^{-rt})\).

title: Logistic growth model — ode_logistic
code: # Evaluate at y = 5 with default parameters (r = 1, K = 10) # dy/dt = 1 * 5 * (1 - 5/10) = 2.5 ode_logistic(t = 0, y = c(5), parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 2.5 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_phase_portrait(ode_logistic, ylim = c(-2, 12)) } # }

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
text: classic two-species predator-prey system:

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
code: ode_lotka_volterra(   t,   y,   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5, delta = 0.5, gamma = 1) )

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous system; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = prey (\(x\)), \(y[2]\) = predator. parameters Named numeric vector elements: alpha Prey growth rate. Default: 1. beta Predation rate. Default: 0.5. delta Predator growth rate per prey consumed. Default: 0.5. gamma Predator death rate. Default: 1.

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((dx/dt, dy/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
text: $$\frac{dx}{dt} = \alpha x - \beta x y$$ $$\frac{dy}{dt} = \delta x y - \gamma y$$ \(x(t)\) prey abundance, \(y(t)\) predator abundance, \(\alpha > 0\) prey growth rate, \(\beta > 0\) predation rate, \(\delta > 0\) predator growth rate per prey consumed, \(\gamma > 0\) predator death rate. system two equilibria: trivial unstable equilibrium \((0, 0)\) neutrally stable center \((x^*, y^*) = (\gamma/\delta,\; \alpha/\beta)\). trajectories starting positive quadrant closed orbits around interior equilibrium.

title: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model — ode_lotka_volterra
code: # Interior equilibrium at (gamma/delta, alpha/beta) = (2, 2) ode_lotka_volterra(t = 0, y = c(2, 2),                   parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5,                                  delta = 0.5, gamma = 1)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 0 0 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_lotka_volterra, xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5)) } # }

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
text: ODE monomolecular (saturating, Mitscherlich) growth:

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
code: ode_monomolecular(t, y, parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10))

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (used directly, included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 1. Current state: \(y[1]\) current value. parameters Named numeric vector elements: r Rate constant. Default: 1. K Asymptote (maximum value). Default: 10.

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
text: list one element: numeric vector length 1 containing \(dy/dt\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
text: $$\frac{dy}{dt} = r \left(K - y\right)$$ \(y(t)\) quantity interest (e.g., biomass), \(r > 0\) rate constant, \(K > 0\) asymptote (maximum attainable value). Growth decelerates monotonically \(y\) approaches \(K\). single stable equilibrium \(y = K\). Unlike logistic growth, inflection point — growth rate greatest \(y = 0\) decreases linearly zero \(y = K\). analytic solution \(y(t) = K(1 - e^{-rt}) + y_0 \, e^{-rt}\).

title: Monomolecular growth model — ode_monomolecular
code: # Evaluate at y = 4 with default parameters (r = 1, K = 10) # dy/dt = 1 * (10 - 4) = 6 ode_monomolecular(t = 0, y = c(4), parameters = c(r = 1, K = 10)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 6 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_phase_portrait(ode_monomolecular, ylim = c(0, 12)) } # }

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
text: Morris-Lecar (1981) conductance-based neuron model, 2D reduction Hodgkin-Huxley dynamics barnacle muscle fiber:

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
code: ode_morris_lecar(   t,   y,   parameters = c(I = 0, C = 20, gCa = 4.4, gK = 8, gL = 2, VCa = 120, VK = -84, VL = -60,     V1 = -1.2, V2 = 18, V3 = 2, V4 = 30, phi = 0.04) )

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = membrane potential \(V\) (mV), \(y[2]\) = K\(^+\) channel open probability \(N\) (dimensionless, \(0 \leq N \leq 1\)). parameters Named numeric vector elements: Applied current (\(\mu\)/cm\(^2\)). Default: 0. C Membrane capacitance (\(\mu\)F/cm\(^2\)). Default: 20. gCa Maximum Ca\(^{2+}\) conductance (mS/cm\(^2\)). Default: 4.4. gK Maximum K\(^+\) conductance (mS/cm\(^2\)). Default: 8. gL Leak conductance (mS/cm\(^2\)). Default: 2. VCa Ca\(^{2+}\) reversal potential (mV). Default: 120. VK K\(^+\) reversal potential (mV). Default: -84. VL Leak reversal potential (mV). Default: -60. V1 Voltage half-activation Ca\(^{2+}\) (mV). Default: -1.2. V2 Slope Ca\(^{2+}\) activation (mV). Default: 18. V3 Voltage half-activation K\(^+\) (mV). Default: 2. V4 Slope K\(^+\) activation (mV). Default: 30. phi Reference frequency (dimensionless). Default: 0.04.

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((dV/dt, dN/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
text: $$C \frac{dV}{dt} = - g_{Ca} M_\infty(V)(V - V_{Ca}) - g_K N (V - V_K) - g_L (V - V_L)$$ $$\frac{dN}{dt} = \phi \frac{N_\infty(V) - N}{\tau_N(V)}$$ voltage-dependent steady-state functions : $$M_\infty(V) = \frac{1}{2}\left(1 + \tanh\!\left(\frac{V - V_1}{V_2}\right)\right)$$ $$N_\infty(V) = \frac{1}{2}\left(1 + \tanh\!\left(\frac{V - V_3}{V_4}\right)\right)$$ $$\tau_N(V) = \left(\cosh\!\left(\frac{V - V_3}{2 V_4}\right)\right)^{-1}$$ state variables membrane potential \(V\) (mV) \(N\), probability K\(^+\) channel open. Depending applied current \(\) parameters, system can exhibit stable resting state, stable limit cycle (repetitive firing), bistability, making rich teaching example bifurcation analysis.

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
text: Morris C, Lecar H (1981). Voltage oscillations barnacle giant muscle fiber. Biophysical Journal 35(1): 193-213. doi:10.1016/S0006-3495(81)84782-0

title: Morris-Lecar neuron model — ode_morris_lecar
code: # At resting potential with zero applied current ode_morris_lecar(t = 0, y = c(-60, 0),                 parameters = c(I = 0, C = 20,                                gCa = 4.4, gK = 8, gL = 2,                                VCa = 120, VK = -84, VL = -60,                                V1 = -1.2, V2 = 18, V3 = 2, V4 = 30,                                phi = 0.04)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 0.057500749 0.000999041 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_morris_lecar,               xlim = c(-80, 60), ylim = c(0, 0.6)) } # }

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
text: simple pendulum written 2D first-order system:

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
code: ode_simple_pendulum(t, y, parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0))

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = angle \(\theta\) (radians), \(y[2]\) = angular velocity \(\omega\). parameters Named numeric vector elements: gL Ratio \(g/L\) (gravitational acceleration divided pendulum length). Default: 1. b Damping coefficient. Default: 0 (undamped).

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((d\theta/dt, d\omega/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
text: $$\frac{d\theta}{dt} = \omega$$ $$\frac{d\omega}{dt} = -\frac{g}{L} \sin(\theta) - b\,\omega$$ \(\theta\) angle vertical (radians), \(\omega\) angular velocity, \(g/L\) ratio gravitational acceleration pendulum length (combined single parameter gL convenience), \(b \geq 0\) damping coefficient. Setting \(b = 0\) gives undamped (conservative) pendulum heteroclinic orbits connecting unstable equilibria \(\theta = \pm\pi\). Adding damping (\(b > 0\)) makes stable equilibrium \(\theta = 0\) stable spiral (small \(b\)) stable node (large \(b\)).

title: Simple pendulum model — ode_simple_pendulum
code: # Undamped pendulum at theta = pi/4, omega = 0 ode_simple_pendulum(t = 0, y = c(pi/4, 0), parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0)) #> [[1]] #> [1]  0.0000000 -0.7071068 #>   # Damped pendulum ode_simple_pendulum(t = 0, y = c(pi/4, 0), parameters = c(gL = 1, b = 0.5)) #> [[1]] #> [1]  0.0000000 -0.7071068 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_simple_pendulum,               xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-3, 3)) } # }

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
text: classic Kermack-McKendrick SIR (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered) epidemic model. Supports two formulations via scale parameter:

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
code: ode_sir(t, y, parameters = c(beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1))

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous system; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = \(S\) (susceptible), \(y[2]\) = \(\) (infected). parameters Named numeric vector elements: beta Transmission rate. Default: 0.5. gamma Recovery rate. Default: 0.1. N Total population size (used scale = "counts"). Default: 1000. scale Character: "proportions" (default) "counts". Can passed named element parameters list, separate argument.

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((dS/dt, dI/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
text: Proportions (scale = "proportions", default): $$\frac{dS}{dt} = -\beta S $$ $$\frac{dI}{dt} = \beta S - \gamma $$ \(S + + R = 1\) times. \(\beta\) transmission rate \(\gamma\) recovery rate. basic reproduction number \(R_0 = \beta / \gamma\). Counts (scale = "counts"): $$\frac{dS}{dt} = -\beta S / N$$ $$\frac{dI}{dt} = \beta S / N - \gamma $$ \(N\) total (constant) population size. proportions formulation recovered setting \(N = 1\). Note: \(S\) \(\) tracked state variables. \(R\) can recovered \(R = N - S - \). scale argument passed part parameters maintain deSolve compatibility. mixing character numeric values c() coerces everything character, pass parameters list() including scale: parameters = list(beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1, N = 1000, scale = "counts"). Passing plain numeric c() vector (without scale) also works recommended form proportions formulation. scale absent, "proportions" assumed.

title: SIR epidemic model — ode_sir
code: # Proportions formulation — plain numeric vector is fine ode_sir(t = 0, y = c(0.99, 0.01),         parameters = c(beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1)) #> [[1]] #> [1] -0.00495  0.00395 #>   # Counts formulation — use list() to avoid character coercion ode_sir(t = 0, y = c(990, 10),         parameters = list(beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1, N = 1000,                           scale = "counts")) #> [[1]] #> [1] -4.95  3.95 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_sir, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) } # }

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
text: Gardner et al. (2000) mutual repressor model synthetic genetic toggle switch:

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
code: ode_toggle(t, y, parameters = c(alpha1 = 3, alpha2 = 3, beta = 2, gamma = 2))

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = \(u\), \(y[2]\) = \(v\). parameters Named numeric vector elements: alpha1 Effective synthesis rate \(u\). Default: 3. alpha2 Effective synthesis rate \(v\). Default: 3. beta Cooperativity coefficient \(u\) equation. Default: 2. gamma Cooperativity coefficient \(v\) equation. Default: 2.

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((du/dt, dv/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
text: $$\frac{du}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_1}{1 + v^\beta} - u$$ $$\frac{dv}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_2}{1 + u^\gamma} - v$$ \(u\) \(v\) concentrations two mutually repressing proteins. \(\alpha_1, \alpha_2 > 0\) effective synthesis rates (incorporating promoter strength degradation), \(\beta, \gamma > 0\) cooperativity (Hill) coefficients. sufficiently large \(\alpha_1\) \(\alpha_2\) cooperativity \(> 1\), system bistable: two stable equilibria (corresponding one protein dominant) separated unstable saddle point. bistability basis synthetic biological memory device.

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
text: Gardner TS, Cantor CR, Collins JJ (2000). Construction genetic toggle switch Escherichia coli. Nature 403: 339–342. doi:10.1038/35002131

title: Genetic toggle switch model — ode_toggle
code: ode_toggle(t = 0, y = c(2, 0.5),            parameters = c(alpha1 = 3, alpha2 = 3, beta = 2, gamma = 2)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 0.4 0.1 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_toggle, xlim = c(0, 4), ylim = c(0, 4)) } # }

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
text: Van der Pol oscillator, nonlinear oscillator self-sustaining oscillations (limit cycle), written 2D first-order system:

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
code: ode_van_der_pol(t, y, parameters = c(mu = 1))

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (autonomous; included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 2. State vector: \(y[1]\) = position \(x\), \(y[2]\) = velocity. parameters Named numeric vector element: mu Nonlinearity parameter. Default: 1. Must \(\geq 0\).

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
text: list one element: numeric vector length 2 containing \((dx/dt, dy/dt)\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
text: $$\frac{dx}{dt} = y$$ $$\frac{dy}{dt} = \mu (1 - x^2) y - x$$ \(x(t)\) position (displacement), \(y(t)\) velocity, \(\mu \geq 0\) nonlinearity/damping parameter. \(\mu = 0\) system reduces harmonic oscillator circular orbits. \(\mu > 0\) stable limit cycle exists; shape becomes increasingly relaxation-oscillator-like \(\mu\) increases. origin \((0, 0)\) unstable spiral \(\mu > 0\).

title: Van der Pol oscillator — ode_van_der_pol
code: # At (x=2, y=0) with mu=1: dx/dt=0, dy/dt = 1*(1-4)*0 - 2 = -2 ode_van_der_pol(t = 0, y = c(2, 0), parameters = c(mu = 1)) #> [[1]] #> [1]  0 -2 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_flow_field(ode_van_der_pol, xlim = c(-4, 4), ylim = c(-4, 4)) } # }

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
text: ODE von Bertalanffy growth, widely used fisheries biology ecology model growth organisms:

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
code: ode_von_bertalanffy(t, y, parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5))

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
text: t Numeric scalar. Time (used directly, included deSolve compatibility). y Numeric vector length 1. Current state: \(y[1]\) body mass (must non-negative). parameters Named numeric vector elements: alpha Anabolism coefficient. Default: 1. beta Catabolism coefficient. Default: 0.5.

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
text: list one element: numeric vector length 1 containing \(dy/dt\), required deSolve::ode().

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
text: $$\frac{dy}{dt} = \alpha \, y^{2/3} - \beta \, y$$ \(y(t)\) body mass (proportional measure body size), \(\alpha > 0\) anabolism coefficient (growth rate proportional surface area, hence \(2/3\) power), \(\beta > 0\) catabolism coefficient (decay proportional mass). model single non-trivial stable equilibrium \(y^* = (\alpha / \beta)^3\), corresponds asymptotic body size \(W_\infty\) fisheries notation. Note \(y^{2/3}\) defined \(y < 0\). Although biological interpretation restricts \(y \geq 0\), guard applied internally — users restrict phase portrait axes accordingly.

title: Von Bertalanffy growth model — ode_von_bertalanffy
code: # Evaluate at y = 8 with default parameters (alpha = 1, beta = 0.5) # dy/dt = 1 * 8^(2/3) - 0.5 * 8 = 4 - 4 = 0  (equilibrium) ode_von_bertalanffy(t = 0, y = c(8), parameters = c(alpha = 1, beta = 0.5)) #> [[1]] #> [1] -4.440892e-16 #>   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gg_phase_portrait(ode_von_bertalanffy, ylim = c(0, 12)) } # }

title: ggphasr 0.0.0.9000
text: Added gg_flow_field() direction/velocity field visualization 1D 2D autonomous ODE systems, support equal-length proportional arrow scaling, magnitude color mapping, multi-system overlay. Added gg_nullclines() plotting x- y-nullclines via contour methods, configurable colors, line types, legend support. Added gg_trajectory() numerically integrated solution paths one initial conditions, forward backward integration, direction arrows, automatic color scaling across trajectories. Added gg_phase_portrait() 1D phase line visualization directional arrows filled/open circle markers stable/unstable equilibria. Added gg_time_series() time-domain plots ODE solutions, faceted panels 2D systems multiple initial condition support. Added gg_manifolds() stable unstable manifold computation visualization saddle points 2D systems. Added gg_phase_plane() high-level --one wrapper producing flow fields, nullclines, trajectories, equilibrium annotations single call. Returns named list $plot $equilibria components. Added add_layer() adding ggplot2 layers ggphasr_result object preserving $equilibria data frame. Added find_equilibrium() numerical equilibrium finding via Newton-Raphson (rootSolve), single-guess grid-search modes. Added classify_equilibrium() trace-determinant stability classification using finite-difference Jacobian. Returns tidy data frame row. Added theme_phase_plane(), ggplot2 theme designed phase plane plots white background, light grey grid lines, centered axes. Added built-ODE systems: four 1D growth models, eight classical 2D systems, fifteen generic textbook examples (ported phaseR). phaseR/deSolve-compatible (Convention ) simplified (Convention B) ODE function signatures supported throughout, automatic detection.
